title: ML Loss Functions: ELC Classification and Cost Analysis
ELC_definition: ELC(R) = analytic, non-oscillatory. Piecewise functions with kinks (non-analytic) are OUTSIDE real ELC.

cost_N_examples.note: sum of N terms requires 2N-2 additions at 2n each

losses.name: Binary Cross-Entropy

cost_per_example.breakdown: ln(y_hat)=1n, mul(y)=2n, sub(1-y_hat)=2n, ln(1-y_hat)=1n, mul(1-y)=2n, add=2n, neg=2n

losses.reason: Analytic for y_hat in (0,1); smooth log composition

losses.name: KL Divergence

cost_N_examples.note: Same structure as Shannon entropy; N terms summed

losses.reason: Analytic for p,q > 0; smooth log and product composition

losses.name: Hinge Loss
losses.reason: max() is piecewise — non-analytic kink at 1-y*y_hat=0

smooth_approximation.name: Softplus hinge
smooth_approximation.note: Smooth approximation; converges to hinge as temperature -> 0

losses.name: Huber Loss
losses.reason: Piecewise-defined — quadratic and linear branches joined at |error|=delta; non-analytic at boundary

smooth_approximation.note: No exact smooth version; pseudo-Huber (sqrt(1+x^2)-1) is a common smooth substitute

losses.name: Focal Loss
losses.reason: Analytic for p > 0; smooth power and log composition

losses.name: Wasserstein-1 (Earth Mover's Distance)
losses.reason: Requires solving a linear program (optimal transport); supremum over function class — not expressible as a closed-form analytic composition
losses.note: WGAN approximates via neural network critic; the critic network output is inside ELC, but the true W1 distance is not

losses.name: Contrastive Loss
losses.reason: The max(0, m-d) term is piecewise — non-analytic at d=m

losses.name: InfoNCE (used in CLIP, SimCLR)

cost_per_example.breakdown: softmax over N keys: N exp evaluations (1n each) + N-1 additions (2n each) + 1 div (1n) + 1 ln (1n)

losses.reason: Composition of exp, sum, div, and log — all analytic; equivalent to cross-entropy applied to softmax similarities

losses.name: Cosine Similarity Loss

cost_per_example.breakdown: dot product (n-dim): n muls (2n each) + (n-1) adds; norms: n muls + adds + sqrt; final div=1n

losses.reason: Analytic for ||a||, ||b|| > 0; smooth composition of polynomial and sqrt

key_finding.title: ELC boundary coincides with smooth/non-smooth optimization landscape boundary
key_finding.insight: Smooth loss functions (analytic compositions) are INSIDE ELC(R). Piecewise losses with non-analytic kinks are OUTSIDE real ELC. This boundary in ML exactly separates differentiable from non-differentiable training objectives — ELC membership predicts whether a loss has a smooth gradient landscape suitable for gradient-based optimization everywhere.
key_finding.implication: ELC(R) provides a theoretical foundation for the practical preference for smooth losses in deep learning: inside-ELC losses guarantee analytic gradients, no subgradient ambiguity, and well-defined Hessians everywhere in their domain.
